Hello, and welcome to an add-on video about Bolt-on Fitness. So when you have, if you chose to bolt-on online training or remote training, hybrid, etc., to your existing clients, so if you have larger clients and you decided to do this as an internal play and bolt it on top, what does the fitness look like because they're already working your facility? Great question. So there is a reason that you should do this, and it's not a BS fluff reason, it's a very, very real reason, and there are two things that you're going to want to track, and that is it. The first is just like the normal plan, you're going to have them track their steps. The reason for that, people will start working out and then decrease their non-exercise activity. And so when they used to get jittery and clean the house and take the trash out and go up the stairs, and things like that, when they are exhausted from their workouts that they are now doing at your facility, it goes down. And so a lot of times your body will self-adjust because it likes to maintain where it's at. And so for us, if we were going to, as a practitioner, we're going to coach this person and help them maintain a deficit that's being created by the exercise that they're doing at our facility, then we need to make sure that they're not, right? And so when we introduce the deficit, and if you've ever coached clients before, when you introduce the deficit, you can watch if you're tracking and not telling them what to do, that their steps and their non-exercise physical activity will slowly go down in order to make up for the deficit that they're creating in their diet. So super important that from the fitness component, the first thing that we're tracking is that we're tracking their steps and making sure that they are maintaining consistent, and it also gives you another variable that you can coach them on outside of the session. So if you have a set amount of sessions that they're doing and you want to increase their activity, you can tell them to increase their steps because you can't increase the duration or the length of the time that they're at your facility for their training session. So this is the primary way that we track the fitness for bolt-on clients. The second way that we do that is just tracking their attendance, the simple stuff, making sure that they actually attend all of the workouts at the facility as planned and that they're doing them intensely. And so if you wanted to take this step further, you could also have them put on a heart rate monitor or something like that that would sync with their phone and you could discuss that at your bi-weekly check-in. So those are the first two are steps, continuing to monitor to them and using them as a variable that you can control outside of the training that they're doing at the facility. Number two is make sure that they're actually attending the workouts at the facility. And then number three, optional, if you so desired and wanted to go over the top, you could monitor their activity levels for the training sessions themselves to make sure that the intensity is not going down as they go into a deficit since most people are trying to lose weight and so we're going to have them deficit. And so those are the variables that we would want to control and keep track of while someone is a client of ours at our facility and doing hybrid. So I hope that consolidates everything for you. The three things, monitor their steps, monitor their attendance and monitor their heart rate, which is optional if you really want to go overboard and be a super-duper-duper practitioner. All right, awesome sauce. Keep being amazing.
